Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents an individual patient whose medical data is being analyzed. This entity stores basic demographic and administrative information for a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

externalUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user from the external authentication system who manages this patient's records. (Relationship: User 1:N Patient)

name.description: The patient's name or a pseudonym/identifier for privacy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the patient record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the patient record was last updated.

AnalysisSession.title: AnalysisSession
AnalysisSession.description: Represents a single instance of a breast cancer AI analysis performed for a specific patient, summarizing the high-level outcome.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AnalysisSession entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient for whom this analysis was conducted. (Relationship: Patient 1:N AnalysisSession)

analysisDate.description: The date and time when the AI analysis was performed.

inputType.description: The type of data used as input for this analysis session.

prediction.description: The AI's final prediction for malignancy (e.g., 'Benign', 'Malignant').

confidence.description: The confidence score of the AI's prediction, a value between 0 and 1.

diagnosticReportId.description: Reference to the DiagnosticReport generated for this analysis session. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:1 DiagnosticReport)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the analysis session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the analysis session record was last updated.

MammogramInput.title: MammogramInput
MammogramInput.description: Stores details related to a mammogram image used as input for an AI analysis session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MammogramInput entity.

analysisSessionId.description: Reference to the AnalysisSession this mammogram image belongs to. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:1 MammogramInput)

imageUrl.description: URL or reference to where the uploaded mammogram image is stored.

imageValidationStatus.description: The status of the CNN image validation (e.g., 'Valid', 'Invalid', 'Pending').

imageValidationDetails.description: Additional details or reasons if the image validation failed (e.g., 'Not grayscale', 'Low variance').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the mammogram input record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the mammogram input record was last updated.

ClinicalInput.title: ClinicalInput
ClinicalInput.description: Stores the clinical data provided as input for an AI analysis session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClinicalInput entity.

analysisSessionId.description: Reference to the AnalysisSession this clinical data belongs to. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:1 ClinicalInput)

age.description: The patient's age at the time of the analysis.

tumorSize.description: The size of the tumor in millimeters (mm).

familyHistory.description: Indicates if there is a family history of breast cancer (true for 'yes', false for 'no').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the clinical input record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the clinical input record was last updated.

PathologyInput.title: PathologyInput
PathologyInput.description: Stores the pathology measurements provided as input for an AI analysis session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PathologyInput entity.

analysisSessionId.description: Reference to the AnalysisSession this pathology data belongs to. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:1 PathologyInput)

radius.description: The mean radius of the tumor cells.

texture.description: The mean texture of the tumor cells.

perimeter.description: The mean perimeter of the tumor cells.

area.description: The mean area of the tumor cells.

smoothness.description: The mean smoothness of the tumor cells.

compactness.description: The mean compactness of the tumor cells.

concavity.description: The mean concavity of the tumor cells.

symmetry.description: The mean symmetry of the tumor cells.

fractalDimension.description: The mean fractal dimension of the tumor cells.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the pathology input record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the pathology input record was last updated.

FeatureImportanceDetail.title: FeatureImportanceDetail
FeatureImportanceDetail.description: Stores individual feature importance scores and values for a specific AI analysis session, indicating what factors influenced the prediction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeatureImportanceDetail entity.

analysisSessionId.description: Reference to the AnalysisSession this feature importance detail belongs to. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:N FeatureImportanceDetail)

featureName.description: The name of the feature (e.g., 'Tumor Size', 'Mean Radius', 'Mass Density').

featureValue.description: The value of the feature for this specific analysis (e.g., '32 mm', '12.5', 'Clustered'). Stored as string to accommodate various data types.

importanceScore.description: The normalized importance score of this feature in the AI's prediction, a value between 0 and 1.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the feature importance record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the feature importance record was last updated.

DiagnosticReport.title: DiagnosticReport
DiagnosticReport.description: Stores the comprehensive, AI-generated professional diagnostic report for an analysis session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiagnosticReport entity.

analysisSessionId.description: Reference to the AnalysisSession this report belongs to. (Relationship: AnalysisSession 1:1 DiagnosticReport)

reportContent.description: The full text content of the AI-generated diagnostic report.

reportGeneratedDate.description: The date and time when this report was generated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the diagnostic report record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the diagnostic report record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores patient records, with each patient explicitly linked to an `externalUserId` (the authenticated user's ID) as its owner. This `externalUserId` is also the `{userId}` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this patient record.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

definition.description: Stores AI analysis sessions for a specific patient. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (the user's ID) and `patientId` for authorization independence and efficient querying, ensuring rules do not need `get()` calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this analysis session.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this analysis session.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the analysis session.

definition.description: Stores mammogram input details for an analysis session. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (the user's ID), `patientId`, and `analysisSessionId` for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this mammogram input record.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this mammogram input record.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis session this mammogram input belongs to.

params.name: mammogramInputId
params.description: The unique identifier for the mammogram input.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this clinical input record.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this clinical input record.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis session this clinical input belongs to.

params.name: clinicalInputId
params.description: The unique identifier for the clinical input.

definition.description: Stores pathology input details for an analysis session. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (the user's ID), `patientId`, and `analysisSessionId` for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this pathology input record.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this pathology input record.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis session this pathology input belongs to.

params.name: pathologyInputId
params.description: The unique identifier for the pathology input.

definition.description: Stores individual feature importance scores for an analysis session. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (the user's ID), `patientId`, and `analysisSessionId` for authorization independence, simplifying security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this feature importance detail.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this feature importance detail.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis session this feature importance detail belongs to.

params.name: featureImportanceDetailId
params.description: The unique identifier for the feature importance detail.

definition.description: Stores the AI-generated diagnostic report for an analysis session. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (the user's ID), `patientId`, and `analysisSessionId` for authorization independence, ensuring robust and simple security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user who owns this diagnostic report.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the patient associated with this diagnostic report.

params.name: analysisSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis session this diagnostic report belongs to.

params.name: diagnosticReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the diagnostic report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a strictly hierarchical, path-based ownership model, starting with individual users. All data for patients and their associated analysis and reports are nested under the authenticated user's ID. This directly addresses **Authorization Independence** and **Clarity of Intent** by ensuring that every document's ownership can be verified directly from its path and/or a denormalized `ownerId` field within the document.

Specifically, the `Patient` entity, which represents the highest level of user-owned application data, resides under `/users/{userId}/patients/{patientId}`. The `Patient` document's `externalUserId` field explicitly identifies its owner, matching the `{userId}` wildcard in its path. This is a core denormalization for the `Patient` entity.

For all subsequent nested entities (`AnalysisSession`, `MammogramInput`, `ClinicalInput`, `PathologyInput`, `FeatureImportanceDetail`, `DiagnosticReport`), the `ownerId` (copied from `Patient.externalUserId`) is denormalized and stored directly within each document. Additionally, relevant parent IDs (`patientId`, `analysisSessionId`) are also denormalized into their child documents. This crucial denormalization strategy eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to traverse parent documents for authorization checks. For example, to check access for a `DiagnosticReport`, rules can simply verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`.

This structure strongly supports **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**. By tightly coupling data to the user's path and denormalizing ownership, `list` operations (e.g., retrieving all `AnalysisSession`s for a `Patient`) are inherently secure. A query to `/users/{userId}/patients/{patientId}/analysisSessions` will only return documents for which `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path (and `resource.data.ownerId` if further rule checks are applied on the document itself). This prevents unauthorized data from being fetched and then filtered by rules, ensuring efficient and secure data access.

**Structural Segregation** is achieved as all data within this user-centric hierarchy shares the same security posture: it is private to the owning user. There are no mixed public/private documents within the same collections. The use of consistent naming (`{userId}`, `{patientId}`) and explicit field definitions further enhances **Data Clarity and Predictability**.
